steps.title: Identify what y represents
steps.content: The variable $y$ represents the total amount of shellfish (in pounds) that the sea otter ate during the entire 6-day period described in the problem.

steps.title: Extract the given information and write the equation
steps.content: The problem states: In 6 days, a sea otter ate 27.0 pounds of shellfish. Since $y$ is defined as the total amount eaten in these 6 days, we directly have $y = 27$.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $y = 27 + 6$: Adding the number of days to the pounds has no meaning here — the total is 27 pounds, not 33. ✗
  • Choice B — $y = 27$: The sea otter ate exactly 27.0 pounds total over the 6 days — $y$ equals that constant. ✓
  • Choice C — $y = \frac{27}{6}$: This gives the average per day (4.5 lb/day), but $y$ represents the total over all 6 days, not the daily rate. ✗
  • Choice D — $y = 6$: 6 is the number of days, not the amount of shellfish eaten. ✗
That is the correct answer.
